seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, tool loops manifest as infinite cycles where agents repeatedly invoke the same tools or handoff between specialized agents without progressing toward task completion. This occurs due to the SDK's flexible agent handoff patterns and lack of built-in loop prevention in the `Runner` execution flow. The problem is exacerbated in multi-agent workflows where `handoff_to` calls create unintended feedback loops between agents with overlapping toolsets.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Runner logs show repeated `handoff_to` calls between AgentA and AgentB with identical tool outputs
howItManifests.impact: Infinite execution consuming API credits without task completion

howItManifests.scenario: Researcher agent repeatedly calls web_search tool
howItManifests.symptom: Same search query executed >5 times in `Runner` trace with identical results
howItManifests.impact: Stuck in tool loop, API rate limits hit, no final answer produced

howItManifests.scenario: Guardrail agent hands back to main agent after validation
howItManifests.symptom: `validate_output()` always returns handoff_to(main_agent), creating ping-pong loop
howItManifests.impact: Guardrail bypassed via repetitive handoffs, security vulnerabilities exposed

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-tool agent cycles between calculator and data_fetcher
howItManifests.symptom: Tool call sequence repeats: `calculate() → fetch_data() → calculate()` endlessly
howItManifests.impact: Compute waste, timeout errors in production deployments

detection.name: Runner Trace Loop Detector
detection.description: Parse OpenAI Agents SDK Runner traces to detect repeated tool calls or handoffs exceeding threshold

detection.name: Live Execution Monitor
detection.description: Monkey-patch Runner.step() to track tool call frequency during execution

detection.name: State Pattern Matcher
detection.description: Detect loops by matching repeating patterns in agent state transitions

fixes.name: Max Tool Calls Per Agent
fixes.description: Configure agents with tool call limits using SDK's built-in constraints
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner

# Create agent with tool call limits
researcher = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    instructions="...",
    tools=[web_search, calculator],
    max_tool_calls={"web_search": 3, "calculator": 5},
    max_iterations=10
)

# Runner with global safeguards
runner = Runner(
    agents=[researcher, validator],
    max_steps=50,
    loop_detection_window=10
)

result = runner.run('query')

fixes.name: Handoff Blacklist
fixes.description: Prevent immediate reverse handoffs between agents

fixes.name: Progressive Tool Escalation
fixes.description: Force agent progression by disabling repeated tools after N calls

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade loop detection as an output guardrail that integrates seamlessly with OpenAI Agents SDK. By adding VexGuardrail to each agent's output pipeline, you automatically detect tool loops, handoff cycles, and state repetition patterns without modifying core agent logic. Vex maintains execution state across multi-agent handoffs and triggers automatic remediation including tool disabling, forced termination, or escalated handoff to a supervisor agent.

The guardrail analyzes Runner traces in real-time using pattern matching and frequency analysis, providing detailed diagnostics including loop length, repeating sequences, and cost impact. Vex's adaptive thresholds learn from your specific agent topologies, reducing false positives while catching subtle loops that static detection misses. Integration requires only 3 lines of code per agent.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Add Vex guardrail to each agent
researcher = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    tools=[web_search],
    output_guardrails=[
        VexGuardrail(
            detector="tool_loop",
            max_tool_calls=5,
            max_handoffs=3,
            remediation="terminate_with_summary"
        )
    ]
)

validator = Agent(
    name="Validator",
    output_guardrails=[
        VexGuardrail(detector="handoff_loop", window_size=10)
    ]
)

# Production runner with Vex
runner = Runner(agents=[researcher, validator], enable_vex_tracing=True)
result = runner.run(user_query)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of VexGuardrail to eliminate tool loops across your entire OpenAI Agents SDK deployment.
